task: EXECUTE-MEASURE the cross-entropy/subword-complexity wildcard on gate2 L6/L7/L8: (a) subword complexity p(n); (b) KL/cross-entropy floor between coexisting arcs; (c) per-ball arc entropy vs rho. Does the data support a cross-entropy/complexity bound that yields arc-incidence O(rho)?
scripts: cross-checked vs design/lemma/route3-arcs-all.json (authoritative full-center arc counts)

a_subword_complexity.finding: p(n) is NEAR-MAXIMAL: essentially every factor of length >= ~8 is distinct (p(8)/L = 308405/311738 = 0.989). Same at L6/L7. The finite realized instruction word is generic/high-complexity, NOT p(n) ~ K*n.
a_subword_complexity.why_not_diagnostic: The linear-complexity theorem (primitive substitution => LR => p(n)<=Kn, Durand) is an ASYMPTOTIC statement about the INFINITE fixed point; it is invisible in the finite window where p(n) saturates to L-n+1 for BOTH curves and blobs. So subword complexity does not discriminate curve from blob here, and its (asymptotic) linearity comes from primitivity of M, twist-irrelevant. Confirms verdict.

b_cross_entropy_floor.finding: The cross-entropy / KL FLOOR between step-distributions of arcs that co-occupy one ball is EXACTLY ZERO. Coexisting arcs routinely appear exactly parallel (angle 0.00 deg, KL 0.0) once stacking is nontrivial (rho>=7). The floor DECREASES to 0 as rho (stacking) grows -- the OPPOSITE of what a capping bound needs. Independently reproduces route1 selfexcl min-coexisting-angle = 0.00 deg on the gate2 chain.
b_cross_entropy_floor.verdict: REFUTED. No positive divergence floor exists; the direction/step-marginal cross-entropy cannot cap arc stacking. Parallel arcs stay triple-free by lateral integer offset (cross>=1, never 0), so zero divergence is geometrically forced, not a coincidence.

c_per_ball_arc_entropy.finding: Per-ball arc-identity entropy is BOUNDED (<= log(#arcs), #arcs bounded ~3-5) and essentially FLAT in rho. The O(rho) crowding (max_c slope ~0.92-0.96, level-uniform L5/L6/L7) is driven by arc LENGTH / sojourn (a single curve pass staying in the ball), NOT by many arcs stacking. Arc-incidence O(rho) DOES hold empirically -- but as the geometric sojourn fact.
c_per_ball_arc_entropy.note_on_R065: My 2500-center sample undersamples the crowding max (gave narcs slope 0.15-0.42); the authoritative full-center max_c slope is ~0.92 (near-linear). Either way the geometry is curve-like, far below blob's slope 3.
c_per_ball_arc_entropy.why_not_a_bridge: Bounded arc-entropy is consistent with arc-incidence but is a RESTATEMENT of it (few arcs, long sojourn = the OSC/refill constant). Since (a) is near-maximal and (b)'s floor is 0, neither information-theoretic quantity can IMPLY the bounded arc-entropy without assuming it. Circular for the tight bound -- the same wall.

overall: The data does NOT support a cross-entropy/complexity bound that yields arc-incidence. It quantitatively CONFIRMS the mirage-or-same-wall verdict on fresh L6/L7/L8: (a) finite-word subword complexity is generic/near-maximal (not curve-diagnostic); (b) the cross-entropy/KL floor is EXACTLY ZERO (exactly-parallel coexisting arcs) -- the cross-entropy cap hypothesis is refuted; (c) the walk IS geometrically curve-like (O(rho) crowding, bounded #arcs), but that is the arc-incidence lemma itself, restated, not something the entropy/complexity quantities produce. Same wall.
outcome: REDUCES-TO-ARC-INCIDENCE (with the specific cross-entropy-floor sub-hypothesis REFUTED at 0)
